steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes a "counterintuitive effect": rising prices caused buyers to throng the market rather than avoid it. The blank completes the phrase describing what rising prices did to demand. The word must mean "produced" or "caused" demand.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘engendering’: means to bring about or produce. Rising prices engendering demand = rising prices creating demand, which matches the counterintuitive reversal described. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘monetizing’: means converting something into a source of profit. It does not describe what happened to the level of demand. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘appraising’: means assessing or estimating value. It has nothing to do with demand being created. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘exploiting’: means taking unfair advantage of something. It does not describe demand being produced. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
